raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need bash command to get build number of current kernel. On Linux, uname -r gives version string like 5.15.0-104-generic. Build number is 104. So we can parse. Command: uname -r | awk -F- '{print $2}'? Actually string: 5.15.0-104-generic. After dash, 104-generic. We want 104. So cut -d- -f2 | cut -d- -f1. Or use sed. So final: uname -r | awk -F- '{print $2}' | cut -d- -f1. Or simpler: uname -r | awk -F- '{print $2}' | sed 's/-.*//'. Let's output one line.
